text: The Graduate Transition and Academic Success (GTAS) Program is a three-week intensive, in-person program held at Queen’s University prior to the Fall semester. Designed for incoming international graduate students, the program introduces academic practices, social integration strategies, essential campus resources, and community connections to support a successful transition to graduate study in Canada.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Through the GTAS Program, participants enhance academic reading, writing, speaking, listening, time management, and academic integrity skills. Students also develop social settlement competencies, gain familiarity with university and community resources in Kingston, and build meaningful connections with peers, faculty, and staff to foster belonging and engagement.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The GTAS Program capacity is limited to 20 students. The 2025 program will run from August 11–29 and is open to incoming international graduate students beginning their academic programs in September 2025 who have received an official acceptance letter from Queen’s University. Applications open May 1, 2025, are reviewed on an ongoing basis beginning May 5, 2025, and close May 30, 2025, or when capacity is reached. Applicants will be notified by June 13, 2025.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: There is no tuition fee for the GTAS Program. However, participants are responsible for medical insurance (UHIP), housing, visa costs, food, groceries, transportation, study materials, and optional excursions. UHIP coverage is mandatory for international students, and associated fees are billed through the Queen’s student account.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The GTAS Program schedule includes academic workshops, immigration sessions, wellness and career development programming, cultural adjustment sessions, collaborative group activities, and community engagement experiences across three structured weeks. Students who attend required academic skills sessions and complete the program receive a Learning Across Cultures Certificate issued by Student Academic Success Services (SASS) and the Queen’s University International Centre (QUIC), though it does not appear on the official academic transcript.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Queen’s University International Centre (QUIC) supports international students through immigration advising, settlement programming, and culturally responsive services. Student Academic Success Services (SASS), located within Student Affairs, provides free and confidential academic skill development support to undergraduate, graduate, and professional students.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Graduate Transition and Academic Success (GTAS) Program includes a structured FAQ section outlining detailed participation, application, financial, and accommodation information for incoming international graduate students at Queen’s University.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Eligibility for the GTAS Program requires that applicants be incoming international graduate students who have received an official acceptance letter from Queen’s University for the upcoming academic year and will begin their academic program in September 2025.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: To apply for the GTAS Program, applicants must complete the following steps: Step 1 – Check eligibility requirements. Step 2 – Prepare and upload their official Queen’s University acceptance letter for the upcoming academic year. Step 3 – Complete and submit the official GTAS application form through the School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs website. Step 4 – Upon submission, applicants will receive a confirmation email acknowledging receipt of their application. Application decisions will be communicated by June 13, 2025.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The GTAS Program runs for three weeks, typically during the last three weeks of August before the Fall semester begins. The 2025 program will be held from August 11–29. A minimum of 80% attendance is mandatory for academic, social, and cultural integration workshops and information sessions. Full participation is strongly recommended to maximize program benefits. Participation in social outings and excursions is optional but encouraged to foster peer engagement and community exploration.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: There is no tuition fee for participation in the GTAS Program. However, participants are responsible for associated costs including University Health Insurance Plan (UHIP) coverage, housing, visa expenses, food, groceries, transportation, optional excursions, and study materials. Applicants are encouraged to consult the tentative budget guidance provided on the program website and plan accordingly.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: UHIP (University Health Insurance Plan) coverage is mandatory for international students. UHIP fees are automatically included in the student’s Queen’s University student account and can be paid through the university’s official payment methods. Students who have already paid UHIP as incoming graduate students do not need to pay separately for the GTAS Program period.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Participants are responsible for securing and paying for their own accommodation during the GTAS Program. Available options in Kingston include Homestay arrangements, off-campus housing, and accommodation listing services. Meal inclusion depends on accommodation type; for example, Homestay options may include certain meals.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Students who attend all required academic skills sessions and complete the GTAS Program will receive a Learning Across Cultures Certificate issued jointly by Student Academic Success Services (SASS) and the Queen’s University International Centre (QUIC). The certificate recognizes participation but does not appear on the official Queen’s academic transcript.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: For further questions regarding the GTAS Program, students may contact the program representative directly at heather.merla@queensu.ca.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs
